Elantra > Body and Frame > Quarter Panel > Service and Repair > Removal and Replacement

QUARTER PANEL REMOVAL AND REPLACEMENT — HYUNDAI ELANTRA 2023
Vehicle: 2023 Hyundai Elantra
Operation: Rear Quarter Panel Replacement

REMOVAL PROCEDURE
Step 1. Disconnect battery negative terminal per Hyundai service procedure.
Step 2. Remove rear interior trim panels, package shelf, and access covers.
Step 3. Disconnect wiring harness connectors and grounding straps.
Step 4. Drill spot welds per Hyundai weld diagram specifications.
Step 5. Remove damaged quarter panel outer skin from vehicle.

WELDING SPECIFICATIONS FOR INSTALLATION
Number of weld points: 36 resistance spot welds (SRSW).
MIG plug welds: 6 locations per weld diagram.
Weld flange preparation: remove zinc coating in weld zone before welding.
Heat affected zone: apply anti-corrosion primer after all welding operations.
Weld nugget diameter: minimum 4mm per Hyundai weld specification.

CORROSION PROTECTION
Apply anti-corrosion primer to all bare metal surfaces before installation.
Apply seam sealer to all mating flanges per Hyundai body sealant specification.
Apply cavity wax injection to enclosed cavities after panel installation.
Apply zinc-based rust preventative coating to all drilled weld holes.
Electrocoat primer must be restored where damaged during removal.

INSTALLATION PROCEDURE
Step 1. Prepare replacement panel with anti-corrosion primer application.
Step 2. Apply seam sealer to all mating flanges per Hyundai specification.
Step 3. Install quarter panel using MIG welding procedure at approved weld points.
Step 4. Verify resistance spot welding per weld schedule for all flange locations.
Step 5. Apply corrosion protection per Hyundai body sealant requirements.
Step 6. Verify panel gap specification per body alignment procedure.
Step 7. Reinstall all interior trim and electrical connectors.

Torque all fasteners to Hyundai specification.
Hyundai requires qualified technician review of all collision repairs.
